title: Contributing to ttmoment
text: Fork package clone onto computer. haven’t done , recommend using usethis::create_from_github("PaulESantos/ttmoment", fork = TRUE). Install development dependencies devtools::install_dev_deps(), make sure package passes R CMD check running devtools::check(). R CMD check doesn’t pass cleanly, ’s good idea ask help continuing. Create Git branch pull request (PR). recommend using usethis::pr_init("brief-description--change"). Make changes, commit git, create PR running usethis::pr_push(), following prompts browser. title PR briefly describe change. body PR contain Fixes #issue-number. user-facing changes, add bullet top NEWS.md (.e. just first header). Follow style described https://style.tidyverse.org/news.html.

title: Contributing to ttmoment
text: New code follow tidyverse style guide. can use styler package apply styles, please don’t restyle code nothing PR. use roxygen2, Markdown syntax, documentation. use testthat unit tests. Contributions test cases included easier accept.

title: tidyttmoment
text: Functional traits key characteristics organisms relate performance, ecology, evolution. distribution functional traits can provide important insights functioning ecosystems responses organisms environmental change. Evaluating moments distribution (.e., mean, variance, skewness, kurtosis) standard approach quantifying shape dispersion distribution. widely used ecological evolutionary research. However, calculating moments functional traits R currently two objects confusing beginners users R. developing tidyttmoment R library allows easy efficient calculation moments, researchers can save time reduce potential errors analyses.

title: tidyttmoment
text: Accurate Calculations: Precisely computes Community-Weighted Mean (CWM), Variance (CWV), Skewness (CWS), Excess Kurtosis (CWK) following robust methodologies established trait scaling literature (Wieczynski et al. 2019, Enquist et al. 2015, Šímová et al. 2018, Metcalfe et al. 2020). Robust NA Handling: Statistically accurate scaling properly omits missing trait records numerators denominators. Tidyverse-ready: Fully designed around dplyr principles supporting unquoted column names (tidy evaluation). Semantic Feedback: Implements user-friendly error messaging using standard cli formatting.

title: tidyttmoment
text: can install development version GitHub :

title: tidyttmoment
text: realistic example demonstrating calculate functional moments ecological dataset containing multiple traits, communities, species abundances:
code: library(tidyttmoment) #> ── Attaching tidyttmoment package ──────────────────────── tidyttmoment 0.0.5 ── #> ✔ fundiversity 1.1.1     ✔ funrar       1.5.0 library(dplyr) #>  #> Adjuntando el paquete: 'dplyr' #> The following objects are masked from 'package:stats': #>  #>     filter, lag #> The following objects are masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union set.seed(42)  # 1. Simulate a trait database (e.g., Specific Leaf Area and Wood Density for 10 species) species_traits <- expand.grid(   species = paste0("sp_", 1:10),   trait = c("SLA", "Wood_Density"),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) |>    mutate(trait_value = runif(n(), min = 5, max = 100))  # 2. Simulate a community survey (abundances across 3 different sites) community_survey <- expand.grid(   comm = c("Forest_A", "Forest_B", "Grassland_C"),   species = paste0("sp_", 1:10),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) |>    mutate(abundance = rpois(n(), lambda = 25)) |>    sample_frac(0.8) # Introduce missing species realistically  # 3. Join the traits with the community abundances ecological_data <- inner_join(community_survey, species_traits,                                by = "species", relationship = "many-to-many")  # 4. Calculate the 4 community-weighted moments simultaneously trait_moments <- tidy_calc_moment(   df = ecological_data,    trait_names = trait,   comm_names = comm,   trait_value = trait_value,   weight = abundance )  print(trait_moments) #> # A tibble: 6 × 6 #>   trait        comm          cwm   cwv      cws    cwk #>   <chr>        <chr>       <dbl> <dbl>    <dbl>  <dbl> #> 1 SLA          Forest_A     68.6  626. -0.922   -0.475 #> 2 SLA          Forest_B     71.2  406. -0.663   -0.595 #> 3 SLA          Grassland_C  67.0  563. -0.825   -0.384 #> 4 Wood_Density Forest_A     60.1  676.  0.00362 -1.05  #> 5 Wood_Density Forest_B     70.1  530. -0.138   -1.43  #> 6 Wood_Density Grassland_C  56.1  586.  0.114   -0.887

title: tidyttmoment
text: tidyttmoment now provides tidy wrappers fundiversity funrar packages, allowing compute advanced functional indices directly tidy long-format data. 1. Functional Diversity Indices Calculate FDis, FRic, FEve, FDiv, Rao’s Q: 2. Functional Rarity Indices Calculate Functional Distinctiveness Uniqueness:
code: # Calculate FDis and Rao's Q for the SLA trait fd_indices <- tidy_calc_diversity(   df = ecological_data |> filter(trait == "SLA"),   comm_names = comm,   sp_names = species,   trait_names = trait,   trait_value = trait_value,   weight = abundance,   index = c("FDis", "RaoQ") )  print(fd_indices) #> # A tibble: 3 × 3 #>   comm         FDis     Q #>   <chr>       <dbl> <dbl> #> 1 Forest_A     19.8  26.6 #> 2 Forest_B     16.3  22.2 #> 3 Grassland_C  18.4  25.8 # Computes distinctiveness (by community) and uniqueness (by species) rarity_indices <- tidy_calc_rarity(   df = ecological_data |> filter(trait == "SLA"),   comm_names = comm,   sp_names = species,   trait_names = trait,   trait_value = trait_value,   weight = abundance )  # Community-level distinctiveness (first 5 rows) print(head(rarity_indices$distinctiveness, 5)) #> # A tibble: 5 × 3 #>   comm     species distinctiveness #>   <chr>    <chr>             <dbl> #> 1 Forest_A sp_1              1.13  #> 2 Forest_A sp_10             0.876 #> 3 Forest_A sp_2              1.20  #> 4 Forest_A sp_3              1.82  #> 5 Forest_A sp_4              0.929  # Species-level uniqueness (first 5 rows) print(head(rarity_indices$uniqueness, 5)) #> # A tibble: 5 × 2 #>   species uniqueness #>   <chr>        <dbl> #> 1 sp_1        0.0856 #> 2 sp_10       0.121  #> 3 sp_2        0.0856 #> 4 sp_3        0.582  #> 5 sp_4        0.324

what: tidyttmoment: Functional Trait Moment Calculation — tidyttmoment-package
title: tidyttmoment: Functional Trait Moment Calculation — tidyttmoment-package
text: Calculates community four 'moments' (mean, variance, skewness, kurtosis) given trait based moments described Wieczynski et al. (2019) doi:10.1073/pnas.1813723116 . functional metrics extremely useful characterizing distribution traits plant community. also provides tidyverse-friendly wrappers seamlessly calculate advanced functional diversity indices (e.g., FDis, Rao's Q) using 'fundiversity' (Grenie et al. 2023 doi:10.1111/ecog.06585 ) functional rarity indices using 'funrar' (Grenie et al. 2017 doi:10.1111/ddi.12629 ). Evaluating community-weighted moments diversity metrics allows researchers evaluate shifts optimal phenotypes understand ecological filtering exactness. Calculates community four 'moments' (mean, variance, skewness, kurtosis) given trait based moments described Wieczynski et al. (2019) doi:10.1073/pnas.1813723116 . functional metrics extremely useful characterizing distribution traits plant community. also provides tidyverse-friendly wrappers seamlessly calculate advanced functional diversity indices (e.g., FDis, Rao's Q) using 'fundiversity' (Grenie et al. 2023 doi:10.1111/ecog.06585 ) functional rarity indices using 'funrar' (Grenie et al. 2017 doi:10.1111/ddi.12629 ). Evaluating community-weighted moments diversity metrics allows researchers evaluate shifts optimal phenotypes understand ecological filtering exactness.

title: tidyttmoment: Functional Trait Moment Calculation — tidyttmoment-package
text: Maintainer: Paul Efren Santos Andrade paulefrens@gmail.com (ORCID) contributors: Macroecology Lab [copyright holder, funder]

what: Calculate functional diversity indices from tidy data — tidy_calc_diversity
title: Calculate functional diversity indices from tidy data — tidy_calc_diversity
text: Wraps fundiversity package calculate functional diversity indices (FRic, FDiv, FEve, FDis, Rao's Q) directly tidy long-format data frame. Automatically handles parallelization future::plan() set.

title: Calculate functional diversity indices from tidy data — tidy_calc_diversity
code: tidy_calc_diversity(   df,   comm_names,   sp_names,   trait_names,   trait_value,   weight,   index = c("FRic", "FDiv", "FEve", "FDis", "RaoQ") )

title: Calculate functional diversity indices from tidy data — tidy_calc_diversity
text: df data frame containing trait community data. comm_names Unquoted column name containing community names/IDs. sp_names Unquoted column name containing species names/IDs. trait_names Unquoted column name containing trait IDs. trait_value Unquoted column name containing actual trait values. weight Unquoted column name containing variable used weight trait values (e.g. abundance). index Character vector indices compute. Options "FRic", "FDiv", "FEve", "FDis", "RaoQ". Default compute .

title: Calculate functional diversity indices from tidy data — tidy_calc_diversity
text: tibble community coordinates selected functional diversity indices.

what: Calculate community weighted mean, variance, skewness, and kurtosis — tidy_calc_moment
title: Calculate community weighted mean, variance, skewness, and kurtosis — tidy_calc_moment
text: Calculates community weighted mean, variance, skewness, excess kurtosis given trait based moments described Wieczynski et al. (2019).

title: Calculate community weighted mean, variance, skewness, and kurtosis — tidy_calc_moment
Calculate community weighted mean, variance, skewness, and kurtosis — tidy_calc_moment
text: df data frame containing trait community data. trait_names Unquoted column name containing trait IDs. comm_names Unquoted column name containing community names/IDs. trait_value Unquoted column name containing actual trait values. weight Unquoted column name containing variable used weight trait values (e.g. abundance).

title: Calculate community weighted mean, variance, skewness, and kurtosis — tidy_calc_moment
text: Returns tibble columns comm, trait, cwm (mean), cwv (variance), cws (skewness), cwk (excess kurtosis).

title: Calculate community weighted mean, variance, skewness, and kurtosis — tidy_calc_moment
text: Enquist, B. J., Norberg, J., Bonser, S. P., Violle, C., Webb, C. T., Henderson, ., ... & Savage, V. M. (2015). Scaling traits ecosystems. Advances Ecological Research, 52, 249-318. Metcalfe, R. J., Ozturk, M., & Pouteau, R. (2020). Using functional traits model annual plant community dynamics. Ecology. Šímová, ., Violle, C., Kraft, N. J., Storch, D., Svenning, J. C., Gallagher, R. V., ... & Enquist, B. J. (2018). Spatial patterns climate relationships major plant traits New World differ woody herbaceous species. Global Ecology Biogeography, 27(8), 895-916. Wieczynski, D. J., Boyle, B., Buzzard, V., Duran, S. M., Henderson, . N., Hulshof, C. M., ... & Savage, V. M. (2019). Climate shapes shifts functional biodiversity forests worldwide. Proceedings National Academy Sciences, 116(2), 587-592.

title: Calculate community weighted mean, variance, skewness, and kurtosis — tidy_calc_moment
code: df <- data.frame(trait = c("height", "height", "weight", "weight"),                  trait_value = c(5, 10, 15, 12),                  abundancia = c(1, 2, 1, 3),                  comm = c("A", "A", "B", "B")) tidy_calc_moment(df, trait_names = trait, comm_names = comm, trait_value = trait_value, weight = abundancia) #> # A tibble: 2 × 6 #>   trait  comm    cwm   cwv    cws    cwk #>   <chr>  <chr> <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl>  <dbl> #> 1 height A      8.33  5.56 -0.707 -1.50  #> 2 weight B     12.8   1.69  1.15  -0.667

what: Convert tidy long data to community and trait matrices — tidy_to_matrices
title: Convert tidy long data to community and trait matrices — tidy_to_matrices
text: Helper function pivot tidy long-format data frame site--species abundance matrix species--trait matrix, required indices like functional diversity rarity.

title: Convert tidy long data to community and trait matrices — tidy_to_matrices
code: tidy_to_matrices(df, comm_names, sp_names, trait_names, trait_value, weight)

title: Convert tidy long data to community and trait matrices — tidy_to_matrices
text: df data frame containing trait community data. comm_names Unquoted column name containing community names/IDs. sp_names Unquoted column name containing species names/IDs. trait_names Unquoted column name containing trait IDs. trait_value Unquoted column name containing actual trait values. weight Unquoted column name containing variable used weight (e.g., abundance).

title: Convert tidy long data to community and trait matrices — tidy_to_matrices
text: list containing site_species (site species matrix) species_traits (species trait matrix).

title: Convert tidy long data to community and trait matrices — tidy_to_matrices
code: df <- data.frame(   comm = c("A", "A", "B", "B"),   species = c("sp1", "sp2", "sp1", "sp3"),   trait = c("height", "height", "height", "height"),   trait_value = c(5, 10, 5, 15),   abundance = c(1, 2, 1, 3) ) tidy_to_matrices(df, comm, species, trait, trait_value, abundance) #> $site_species #>   sp1 sp2 sp3 #> A   1   2   0 #> B   1   0   3 #>  #> $species_traits #>     height #> sp1      5 #> sp2     10 #> sp3     15 #>
